understanding.function: Nuclear fuel reprocessing system for extracting fissile material (uranium and plutonium) from spent nuclear fuel waste, capable of processing multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with 1800 tHM/year capacity
understanding.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing and fissile material recovery
constraints: Product purity standards for downstream fuel fabrication
uncertainties: Specific technical processes and equipment details not defined
Exact safety and regulatory requirements beyond ALARA principle
Interface specifications with upstream waste reception and downstream fuel fabrication systems
Detailed operational parameters and control systems
Emergency response and accident scenarios not described

candidates.name: PUREX Process
candidates.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction process - the dominant nuclear fuel reprocessing technology worldwide using tributyl phosphate (TBP) for selective recovery of uranium and plutonium through multiple chemical separation stages
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing
candidates.function_alignment: Direct match - designed for uranium and plutonium extraction from spent nuclear fuel with industrial scale capability
candidates.notes: Most established technology, uses TBP extractant, capable of processing UOX and MOX fuels

candidates.name: Tributyl Phosphate (TBP) Solvent Extraction System
candidates.description: Key extractant used in nuclear fuel reprocessing for selective separation of actinides, typically dissolved in kerosene diluent for liquid-liquid extraction processes
candidates.function_alignment: Core technology component - primary extractant for uranium and plutonium separation with proven industrial application
candidates.notes: Well-established chemistry, high extraction efficiency, compatible with multiple fuel types

candidates.name: Alternative Diluent Systems (Undecanones)
candidates.description: Advanced diluent systems using 2-undecanone as alternative to traditional kerosene for various extraction ligands including TBP, offering improved extraction properties
candidates.function_alignment: Process enhancement - improved extraction efficiency for uranium and plutonium recovery
candidates.notes: Newer technology with potential for higher extraction performance than conventional systems

candidates.name: Direct Oxide Dissolution in Organic Solvents
candidates.description: Alternative reprocessing approach involving direct dissolution of actinide oxides in tributyl phosphate, eliminating aqueous dissolution step and reducing facility requirements
candidates.function_alignment: Alternative approach - potentially more efficient process for uranium and plutonium recovery with reduced facility size
candidates.notes: Emerging technology that could reduce nitrate requirements and simplify process flowsheet

candidates.name: Enhanced Selectivity Systems with AHA
candidates.description: PUREX process enhancement using acetohydroxamic acid (AHA) to improve selectivity in uranium and plutonium separation, with TBP extraction for acid recovery
candidates.function_alignment: Process improvement - enhanced selectivity for fissile material recovery in reprocessing operations
candidates.notes: Addresses chemical equilibrium issues and extraction efficiency optimization

candidates.name: Advanced Amide Extractants (DEHBA/DEHiBA)
candidates.description: Specialized extractant ligands N,N-di-(2-ethylhexyl)butyramide and N,N-di-2-ethylhexylisobutryamide developed for uranium recovery with improved radiation resistance
candidates.function_alignment: Alternative extractant technology - designed for uranium recovery from spent nuclear fuel with enhanced radiation stability
candidates.notes: Developed for high-radiation environments, potential alternative to TBP systems

candidates.name: Uranium-Lanthanide Co-extraction Systems
candidates.description: Direct extraction technology for simultaneous recovery of uranium and lanthanide oxides using tributyl phosphate, applicable to mixed oxide fuel processing
candidates.function_alignment: Specialized application - suitable for MOX fuel processing with simultaneous actinide and lanthanide recovery
candidates.notes: Relevant for MOX fuel reprocessing capability requirement

candidates.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Established aqueous reprocessing technology using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely implemented at industrial scale including La Hague facility.
candidates.function_alignment: Direct - primary industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with proven multi-fuel capability
candidates.notes: Most mature technology for large-scale reprocessing, forms basis of most commercial facilities

candidates.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, designed to reduce proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing capability for MOX fuel cycles.
candidates.function_alignment: High - specifically designed for MOX fuel reprocessing with enhanced security features
candidates.notes: Developed by CEA France, addresses proliferation concerns while maintaining commercial viability

candidates.name: UREX+ Process Suite
candidates.description: Family of aqueous separation processes including UREX+1, UREX+2, UREX+3 variants for selective separation of uranium, transuranics, and fission products with varying degrees of partitioning.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced aqueous separations
candidates.function_alignment: High - designed for multiple fuel types with flexible separation schemes
candidates.notes: US DOE developed processes offering flexibility in product streams and waste minimization

candidates.name: Pyroprocessing (Electrochemical Reprocessing)
candidates.description: High-temperature electrochemical separation process using molten salt electrolytes to separate actinides from fission products, particularly suited for fast reactor fuels and proliferation-resistant operations.
candidates.function_alignment: Medium - alternative technology for actinide recovery, limited commercial scale experience
candidates.notes: Inherently proliferation-resistant but requires different infrastructure than aqueous processes

candidates.name: GANEX (Grouped Actinide Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Advanced aqueous process for co-extraction of all actinides (U, Np, Pu, Am, Cm) using novel extractants, designed for Generation IV fuel cycle applications with enhanced waste minimization.
candidates.function_alignment: Medium - research-stage process for comprehensive actinide recovery
candidates.notes: European development program, not yet at commercial scale but addresses future fuel cycle needs

candidates.name: DIAMEX-SANEX Process Combination
candidates.description: Two-stage aqueous separation process: DIAMEX for trivalent actinide/lanthanide extraction followed by SANEX for selective actinide recovery, enabling minor actinide recycling.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - minor actinide partitioning
candidates.function_alignment: Medium - complementary to main reprocessing for comprehensive waste management
candidates.notes: Addresses long-term waste radiotoxicity through minor actinide separation and transmutation

candidates.name: Fluoride Volatility Process
candidates.description: Gas-phase separation process using fluorination to volatilize uranium as UF6 while leaving plutonium and fission products as non-volatile fluorides, offering potential for simplified remote operations.
candidates.function_alignment: Low - limited to uranium recovery, not suitable for plutonium extraction
candidates.notes: Historical process with limited modern application due to plutonium recovery limitations

candidates.name: ALSEP (Actinide Lanthanide Separation) Process
candidates.description: Selective separation process using bis-triazinyl-pyridine (BTP) extractants for separating trivalent actinides from lanthanides, enabling minor actinide recycling in advanced fuel cycles.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - selective actinide separation
candidates.function_alignment: Low - specialized for minor actinide separation rather than main U/Pu recovery
candidates.notes: Research-stage process for advanced fuel cycle applications with transmutation capability

enriched.name: PUREX Process
enriched.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction process - the dominant nuclear fuel reprocessing technology worldwide using tributyl phosphate (TBP) for selective recovery of uranium and plutonium through multiple chemical separation stages
enriched.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing
enriched.function_alignment: Direct match - designed for uranium and plutonium extraction from spent nuclear fuel with industrial scale capability
enriched.notes: Most established and proven technology, currently operating at required scale worldwide including similar facilities
enriched.gap_analysis: Direct match for function requirements - proven technology for uranium/plutonium extraction at industrial scale with multi-fuel capability. Meets all capacity and processing requirements.

enriched.name: Tributyl Phosphate (TBP) Solvent Extraction System
enriched.description: Key extractant used in nuclear fuel reprocessing for selective separation of actinides, typically dissolved in kerosene diluent for liquid-liquid extraction processes
enriched.function_alignment: Core technology component - primary extractant for uranium and plutonium separation with proven industrial application
enriched.notes: Essential component of PUREX process, mature technology with extensive operational history
enriched.gap_analysis: Core component technology for the required function. Well-established chemistry with proven industrial application for uranium and plutonium separation.

enriched.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
enriched.description: Established aqueous reprocessing technology using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely implemented at industrial scale including La Hague facility.
enriched.function_alignment: Direct - primary industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with proven multi-fuel capability
enriched.notes: Duplicate of first entry but with specific La Hague reference, confirming applicability to French context
enriched.gap_analysis: Perfect alignment with requirements - proven at 1800 tHM/year scale with multi-fuel capability. La Hague reference directly applicable to French jurisdiction.

enriched.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
enriched.description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, designed to reduce proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing capability for MOX fuel cycles.
enriched.function_alignment: High - specifically designed for MOX fuel reprocessing with enhanced security features
enriched.notes: French-developed technology specifically addressing MOX fuel reprocessing with security enhancements
enriched.gap_analysis: Good alignment for MOX fuel processing requirement with enhanced security features. Based on proven PUREX technology but with modifications for proliferation resistance.

enriched.name: Enhanced Selectivity Systems with AHA
enriched.description: PUREX process enhancement using acetohydroxamic acid (AHA) to improve selectivity in uranium and plutonium separation, with TBP extraction for acid recovery
enriched.function_alignment: Process improvement - enhanced selectivity for fissile material recovery in reprocessing operations
enriched.notes: Optimization technology that could improve product purity standards and operational efficiency
enriched.gap_analysis: Process enhancement that improves selectivity and product purity for fissile material recovery. Builds on established PUREX technology.

enriched.name: UREX+ Process Suite
enriched.description: Family of aqueous separation processes including UREX+1, UREX+2, UREX+3 variants for selective separation of uranium, transuranics, and fission products with varying degrees of partitioning.
enriched.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced aqueous separations
enriched.function_alignment: High - designed for multiple fuel types with flexible separation schemes
enriched.notes: Advanced technology offering flexibility but requiring adaptation from US development to French regulatory context
enriched.gap_analysis: Meets function requirements with flexible separation schemes for multiple fuel types. Less mature than PUREX but offers enhanced waste management capabilities.

enriched.name: Uranium-Lanthanide Co-extraction Systems
enriched.description: Direct extraction technology for simultaneous recovery of uranium and lanthanide oxides using tributyl phosphate, applicable to mixed oxide fuel processing
enriched.function_alignment: Specialized application - suitable for MOX fuel processing with simultaneous actinide and lanthanide recovery
enriched.notes: Specialized for MOX fuels but incomplete solution for full fissile material recovery requirements
enriched.gap_analysis: Addresses MOX fuel processing requirement but focuses on uranium recovery rather than comprehensive uranium/plutonium extraction. Specialized application with limited scope.

enriched.name: Alternative Diluent Systems (Undecanones)
enriched.description: Advanced diluent systems using 2-undecanone as alternative to traditional kerosene for various extraction ligands including TBP, offering improved extraction properties
enriched.function_alignment: Process enhancement - improved extraction efficiency for uranium and plutonium recovery
enriched.notes: Promising enhancement technology but requires significant development and validation for industrial scale
enriched.gap_analysis: Process enhancement component that could improve extraction efficiency but requires integration with established reprocessing systems. Limited industrial experience.

enriched.name: Advanced Amide Extractants (DEHBA/DEHiBA)
enriched.description: Specialized extractant ligands N,N-di-(2-ethylhexyl)butyramide and N,N-di-2-ethylhexylisobutryamide developed for uranium recovery with improved radiation resistance
enriched.function_alignment: Alternative extractant technology - designed for uranium recovery from spent nuclear fuel with enhanced radiation stability
enriched.notes: Promising for high-radiation environments but incomplete for comprehensive fissile material recovery
enriched.gap_analysis: Addresses uranium recovery but limited evidence for plutonium extraction capability. Enhanced radiation resistance beneficial but incomplete solution for full requirements.

enriched.name: Direct Oxide Dissolution in Organic Solvents
enriched.description: Alternative reprocessing approach involving direct dissolution of actinide oxides in tributyl phosphate, eliminating aqueous dissolution step and reducing facility requirements
enriched.function_alignment: Alternative approach - potentially more efficient process for uranium and plutonium recovery with reduced facility size
enriched.notes: Innovative approach requiring extensive development and validation before industrial implementation
enriched.gap_analysis: Alternative approach with potential benefits but limited industrial validation. Significant departure from established technology with uncertain scalability to 1800 tHM/year.

enriched.name: Pyroprocessing (Electrochemical Reprocessing)
enriched.description: High-temperature electrochemical separation process using molten salt electrolytes to separate actinides from fission products, particularly suited for fast reactor fuels and proliferation-resistant operations.
enriched.function_alignment: Medium - alternative technology for actinide recovery, limited commercial scale experience
enriched.notes: Fundamentally different technology requiring specialized infrastructure, limited applicability to stated fuel types
enriched.gap_analysis: Alternative technology for actinide recovery but limited commercial scale experience and different infrastructure requirements. Not optimized for UOX/MOX fuel processing.

enriched.name: GANEX (Grouped Actinide Extraction) Process
enriched.description: Advanced aqueous process for co-extraction of all actinides (U, Np, Pu, Am, Cm) using novel extractants, designed for Generation IV fuel cycle applications with enhanced waste minimization.
enriched.function_alignment: Medium - research-stage process for comprehensive actinide recovery
enriched.notes: Advanced research technology not ready for immediate industrial implementation at required scale
